hypothesis: Two-stage repair might preserve the readout-only delayed-route basin while lifting content with a later sink/readout reopening.
retirement_reason: Invalid run: config-level resume was ignored before the resume override fix, so this started from scratch.
notes: This run looked like a hard negative, but it is not scientifically valid because the old main() logic overwrote YAML resume with null unless --resume was passed. It started from step 0 and should only be used as the clue that exposed the resume bug.

hypothesis: With resume semantics fixed, two-stage sink/readout repair from the readout-only preservation source may improve accuracy while keeping enough late-route structure alive.
notes: Corrected resume run started from checkpoint step 23 and preserved the basin while lifting base accuracy. Best val=0.4917/0.4468/0.2701/0.2443/86.96, independent base verify=0.4915/0.4508/0.2493/0.2346/87.86. This is the first scientifically valid two-stage direct-entry repair in the partial-init family.

hypothesis: If memory is the real repair lever after preserving the basin with readout-only training, a second stage that reopens only memory should preserve routing better than sink-side reopening.
notes: Active scout. First meaningful validation at step 119 was 0.1978/0.4604/0.2592/0.2651/90.01 with premature_exit_rate 0.5650, which preserves route slightly better than the sink/readout branch but is much weaker on overall accuracy so far.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the corrected two-stage sink/readout repair is reproducible before promoting it further.
notes: Exact rerun matched the corrected two-stage repair bit-for-bit on best-val and base verify. Best val again was 0.4917/0.4468/0.2701/0.2443/86.96, and independent base verify reproduced at about 0.4915/0.4508/0.2493/0.2346/87.86.
